_source: Kurczak & Fish, Art & Science of Vedic Astrology Vol I, Ch.4 'The Planets' (natural/naisargika karakas) + Ch.7 house-karaka table (p66-90).
_note: The natural significator (naisargika karaka) of each planet — what person/theme it stands for regardless of placement. This is the SIGNIFICATOR side of the knot detector: to test whether a life-event theme (marriage/children/career/fame) is lit, check its house AND its karaka planet(s).
_partnerKarakaRule: Venus is the UNIVERSAL karaka of marriage & the spouse for every chart (the 7th-house karaka). The supplementary partner-significator is Jupiter = the husband-principle, Venus = the wife-principle. Classical texts (K&F included) assign these by the NATIVE's gender because they assume a heterosexual union — Velea corrects this: the partner karaka follows the SPOUSE'S gender, not the native's. A gay man's or straight woman's chart reads the husband through Jupiter; a lesbian woman's or straight man's chart reads the wife through Venus. When orientation/partner is unknown, fall back to the classical opposite-gender default but hold it loosely.

signifies: the ego projected onto the outside world

persons: the husband (the husband-principle — read whenever the spouse is a husband, of any native)
the wife (the wife-principle — read whenever the spouse is a wife, of any native)

houseKarakaTable._note: Both Parashara and Jaimini give only the FIRST-listed planet per house as the single karaka; the extras are secondary.

knotSignificatorMap._note: Derived index for the knot detector — the house + karaka(s) that must be lit for each life-event theme. Gendered where the canon is gendered. Read the theme as ON only when house AND karaka converge (transit/dasha), not either alone.

marriage._note: Venus is always the marriage karaka. Add the partner karaka by the SPOUSE's gender (husband→Jupiter, wife→Venus) — NOT the native's. See _partnerKarakaRule; never assume heterosexuality.

identity._note: identity = self (1st) received in the world (10th/dharma); its expression is chart-specific (career OR marriage OR parenthood)

fame._note: Sun = fame/status/recognition; 10th = public honour; 1st = personal recognition/status
